commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8ae640c75f0542628be7a17f6e9577c91af2835b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional legacy sidecar SQLite import to memory schema setup, passes `memory/<agentId>.sqlite` from the memory-core manager, and adds a sidecar-import regression test.

PR surface: Source +78, Tests +84. Total +162 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main routes memory to the per-agent SQLite DB and only migrates legacy tables inside the opened DB, while https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95495 provides upgrade evidence that the old sidecar remains intact but inactive.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the blocker is the approved migration owner and upgrade policy for shipped memory state, not a narrow automatable edit.

target.title: fix #95495: [Bug]: 2026.6.9 silently relocates memory store with no migration, forcing a full re-embed (1499 files) with zero upgrade-time warning
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the memory index upgrade path from the legacy sidecar database (`memory/<agentId>.sqlite`) to the per-agent SQLite database (`agents/<agentId>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`).
- Imports legacy `meta`, `files`, `chunks`, and `embedding_cache` rows into the canonical memory index tables when the new per-agent DB has no existing memory index rows.
- Keeps the existing same-database legacy table migration intact and skips sidecar import once the new canonical index already has rows, so users who already rebuilt into the new DB are not overwritten.

Closes #95495

- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix for the memory index migration/upgrade path.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High — the failing regression test reproduced the empty per-agent DB symptom, the fix runs at the schema/source-of-truth initialization boundary, and the after-fix runtime proof verified copied rows through the real manager path.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users upgrading from the sidecar memory DB layout keep their existing indexed memories instead of seeing an empty per-agent memory index and needing to re-embed their corpus from scratch.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent root cause. This PR fixes #95495 directly rather than following or completing a merged PR.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `v2026.6.9` moved the built-in memory store from the sidecar path to the per-agent SQLite database, but schema initialization only migrated legacy tables that already existed inside the currently opened database. Runtime startup opened only the new per-agent DB, so existing `memory/main.sqlite` sidecar indexes were left on disk and never attached or imported, causing upgraded users to observe an empty canonical memory index.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch changes the source schema/migration boundary that initializes the canonical memory index: it attaches the historical sidecar database, validates the expected legacy schema, copies rows into the canonical tables, and has the memory-core manager pass the historical sidecar path. That fixes the upgrade invariant at the owner of the persisted index instead of masking search/status behavior after the index has already been lost.

## What changed

- `ensureMemoryIndexSchema()` now accepts an optional legacy sidecar database path and imports it only when the canonical memory index in the current DB is still empty.
- The memory-core manager passes the historical `resolveStateDir(...)/memory/<agentId>.sqlite` sidecar path during schema initialization.
- The schema copy path now shares the same row-copy and conflict checks between same-DB legacy table migration and cross-file sidecar import.
- Added a regression test that seeds a legacy sidecar SQLite file, opens a new agent DB, and verifies sources, chunks, FTS rows, and embedding cache rows are imported.

## Contract surface / compatibility

- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The contract surface is the persisted memory index schema and migration path. The canonical source-of-truth remains the per-agent `openclaw-agent.sqlite` memory index; the legacy sidecar is read during migration only when the canonical index is empty. Compatibility/default behavior is additive for upgrades because existing canonical rows win and no default search semantics, config option, or user-facing API is expanded.
- **What did NOT change:** Out of scope / not changed: secrets/auth stores, QMD export caches, session transcript indexing, provider routing, memory search ranking, embedding model selection, and any already rebuilt per-agent memory index. The patch only imports a legacy sidecar into an empty canonical memory index and does not delete or mutate the old sidecar file.
- Related open PR scan: #94498 and #77158 were reviewed as same-contract/adjacent persistence work and neither imports legacy built-in memory index rows from `memory/<agentId>.sqlite`; details are in the competition section below.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- #94498 (`fix(secrets): commit auth-stores before config to prevent half-migrated credentials`) is not a canonical PR for this issue. It addresses `secrets apply` credential source-of-truth ordering in `src/secrets/apply.ts` after auth stores moved into per-agent SQLite. It does not touch the memory index schema, memory-core manager startup, or the legacy `memory/<agentId>.sqlite` sidecar used by built-in memory search.
- #77158 (`perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions`) is also not a canonical PR for this issue. It adds a QMD session export cache table in `openclaw-agent.sqlite` for performance. It does not import legacy built-in memory index rows from `memory/main.sqlite`, and it does not change the memory index `meta/files/chunks` upgrade path.
- This patch is the narrower source-of-truth fix for #95495 because it changes the memory index schema initialization and the memory-core manager path that actually opens the new per-agent memory database. It leaves secrets/auth-store ordering and QMD export caching untouched.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** An upgraded agent with an existing legacy `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/main.sqlite` memory index and a new empty `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite` must keep its indexed memory rows after startup/status opens the per-agent DB. Before this patch, the new DB stayed empty because only the new path was opened.

- **Real environment tested:** Current PR head `8ae640c75f0542628be7a17f6e9577c91af2835b` in the issue worktree on Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, with an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. The proof used the real `MemoryIndexManager` runtime path with `purpose: "status"`, real schema initialization, and real SQLite files; it did not use a mocked memory manager.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the inline runtime proof with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`. The driver created a temp workspace, seeded `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/main.sqlite` with legacy `meta`, `files`, `chunks`, and `embedding_cache` tables, started `MemoryIndexManager.get({ agentId: "main", purpose: "status" })`, closed the manager, then opened `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite` read-only to inspect the imported canonical memory tables.

- **Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "legacyPath": "/tmp/openclaw-95495-proof-BOuNRo/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite",
  "agentPath": "/tmp/openclaw-95495-proof-BOuNRo/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite",
  "statusFiles": 1,
  "statusChunks": 1,
  "counts": {
    "sources": 1,
    "chunks": 1,
    "cache": 1
  },
  "imported": {
    "id": "legacy-chunk-1",
    "path": "MEMORY.md",
    "text": "remember imported sidecar row"
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The manager status saw `files=1` and `chunks=1`, and the new per-agent database contained the legacy source row, chunk row, and embedding cache row. This proves the old sidecar index is imported through the runtime manager/schema path instead of being ignored.

- **What was not tested:** I did not run the reporter's full 1,499-file memory corpus or any network embedding calls. The proof intentionally focuses on local SQLite upgrade behavior because the bug is the persisted store path/migration gap; imported embeddings and cache rows are verified directly in the new per-agent database.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts` adds a regression test for importing a legacy sidecar memory database into a new agent database.
- **Scenario locked in:** The test creates `memory/main.sqlite` with legacy `meta`, `files`, `chunks`, and `embedding_cache` rows, opens a fresh `agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`, runs `ensureMemoryIndexSchema()`, and asserts canonical sources, chunks, FTS rows, and embedding cache rows are present.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression fixture exercises the schema migration boundary where the bug occurred, without depending on a full memory corpus or network calls, and it would fail if a future refactor again stopped attaching/importing the legacy sidecar before creating the per-agent index.

## Verification

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts
# 7 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.test.ts
# 27 passed

pnpm tsgo:core:test
# exit 0

pnpm tsgo:core
# exit 0

pnpm exec oxfmt --check packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts
# All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
# exit 0
```

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** compatibility, schema, migration, session-state, availability, security-boundary, and auth-provider were considered because this patch touches persisted SQLite schema setup and copies cached embedding rows keyed by provider/model/hash.
- **Risk explanation:** Merge risk is bounded to the memory index upgrade path. The sidecar import only runs when the canonical per-agent memory index is empty; if a user already rebuilt or has any canonical meta/source/chunk rows, the sidecar is not imported and existing canonical data remains the source of truth. The legacy file is attached only to copy validated tables and is detached afterward.
- **Why acceptable:** The compatibility risk is acceptable because the patch restores the expected migration behavior for upgraded users while preserving current default behavior for fresh installs and already-migrated agents. Patch quality warning: the added broad `catch` is a savepoint rollback pattern that releases the savepoint and rethrows the original error; it is not a fallback, silent recovery, or symptom mask.
- **Patch quality notes:** The diff is focused on the memory schema owner, the runtime manager path that supplies the legacy sidecar location, and one regression test. The conflict checks intentionally throw if `INSERT OR IGNORE` cannot faithfully copy legacy rows, so the code fails loudly instead of hiding corrupt or incompatible legacy data.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
